questions.question: What does the word 'geometry' mean?
questions.answer: Geometry means measurement of the earth.

questions.question: From which language is the word geometry derived?
questions.answer: The word geometry is derived from the Greek language.

questions.question: Who is known for organising geometry into a systematic study?
questions.answer: Euclid organised geometry into a systematic study.

questions.question: What is the name of Euclid’s famous book?
questions.answer: Euclid’s famous book is called 'Elements'.

questions.question: How many books are there in Euclid’s Elements?
questions.answer: Euclid’s Elements consists of 13 books.

questions.question: Name the three undefined terms in geometry.
questions.answer: The three undefined terms are point, line and plane.

questions.question: What is an axiom?
questions.answer: An axiom is a statement accepted as true without proof.

questions.question: What is a postulate?
questions.answer: A postulate is an assumption accepted without proof and specific to geometry.

questions.question: How many postulates did Euclid propose?
questions.answer: Euclid proposed five postulates.

questions.question: What is a theorem?
questions.answer: A theorem is a mathematical statement that has been proved.

questions.question: What is the dimension of a point?
questions.answer: A point has zero dimension.

questions.question: How many dimensions does a line have?
questions.answer: A line has one dimension.

questions.question: How many dimensions does a surface have?
questions.answer: A surface has two dimensions.

questions.question: How many dimensions does a solid have?
questions.answer: A solid has three dimensions.

questions.question: State Euclid’s first postulate.
questions.answer: A straight line may be drawn from any one point to any other point.

questions.question: State Euclid’s second postulate.
questions.answer: A terminated line can be produced indefinitely.

questions.question: State Euclid’s third postulate.
questions.answer: A circle can be drawn with any centre and any radius.

questions.question: State Euclid’s fourth postulate.
questions.answer: All right angles are equal to one another.

questions.question: What does the axiom 'things equal to the same thing are equal to one another' mean?
questions.answer: If two quantities are equal to the same quantity, they are equal to each other.

questions.question: What does the axiom 'the whole is greater than the part' state?
questions.answer: A complete quantity is always greater than any of its parts.

questions.question: How many lines pass through two distinct points?
questions.answer: Only one unique line passes through two distinct points.

questions.question: What is a line segment?
questions.answer: A line segment is a part of a line with two endpoints.

questions.question: What is deductive reasoning?
questions.answer: Deductive reasoning is the process of deriving conclusions logically from known facts.

questions.question: What is meant by consistency of axioms?
questions.answer: A system of axioms is consistent if no contradiction arises from them.

questions.question: What does the theorem 'two distinct lines cannot have more than one point in common' mean?
questions.answer: Two different lines can intersect at most at one point.
